concerns.text: T028 places VIF sensitivity analysis (re-training and comparison) in `code/02_features.py`. This violates FR-008 and the Plan's explicit mapping which requires this logic to occur in `code/03_train.py` to separate feature engineering from model validation. The task deliverable falls short of the FR's semantic requirement for a model-validation phase analysis.

T023 tests VIF logic in `tests/test_features.py`. Since FR-008 requires a sensitivity analysis involving *re-training* the model to compare performance metrics, a unit test in the feature module cannot verify the core requirement (the model performance change). This task fails to provide a verification step for the full FR-008 acceptance criterion.

T019 implements 'Data Insufficiency' halt logic (N < 100) in `code/01_ingest.py`. The Spec (Edge Cases) and Plan (Power Analysis) define this threshold as a trigger for the *training* pipeline to halt. Placing this check in the ingestion script (US1) creates a semantic drift: if data is insufficient, the pipeline halts before feature engineering (US2) or training (US3) can even be attempted, potentially violating the 'Independent Test' requirement for US2 and US3 which assume data availability for their specific logic validation.

T027 implements Fox/Gordon-Taylor equations 'as baselines only'. The Spec (FR-004) mandates these be computed as 'interaction features' (predictors) AND as baselines. The task description 'as baselines only' semantically weakens the requirement to generate them as model inputs, risking a failure to deliver the 'interaction features' required by FR-004.
